name: ucdp-regf

metadata.name: ucdp-regf
metadata.summary: Unified Chip Design Platform - Register File

documentation:
[![PyPI Version](https://badge.fury.io/py/ucdp-regf.svg)](https://badge.fury.io/py/ucdp-regf)
[![Python Build](https://github.com/nbiotcloud/ucdp-regf/actions/workflows/main.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/nbiotcloud/ucdp-regf/actions/workflows/main.yml)
[![Documentation](https://readthedocs.org/projects/ucdp-regf/badge/?version=stable)](https://ucdp-regf.readthedocs.io/en/stable/)
[![Coverage Status](https://coveralls.io/repos/github/nbiotcloud/ucdp-regf/badge.svg?branch=main)](https://coveralls.io/github/nbiotcloud/ucdp-regf?branch=main)
[![versions](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/ucdp-regf.svg)](https://github.com/nbiotcloud/ucdp-regf)

# Unified Chip Design Platform - Register File

* [Documentation](https://ucdp-regf.readthedocs.io/en/stable/)
* [PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/ucdp-regf/)
* [Sources](https://github.com/nbiotcloud/ucdp-regf)
* [Issues](https://github.com/nbiotcloud/ucdp-regf/issues)

## Installation

Installing it is pretty easy:

```bash
pip install ucdp-regf
```

Clock Gate.

Example of a simple static module.

The name of the hardware module is derived from the class name:

ClkGateMod ==> clk_gate

classes.name: ucdp_regf.examples.simple.glbl.bus.AddrType
documentation:
Address Type.

Example Bus Address Type Definition, which is fixed to 32-bit.

classes.name: ucdp_regf.examples.simple.glbl.bus.DataType
documentation:
Data Type.

Example Bus Data Type Definition, which has a variable width (32 by default).

classes.name: ucdp_regf.examples.simple.glbl.bus.TransType
documentation:
Transport Type.

Example Bus Transport Indication.

This definition is an enumeration.

classes.name: ucdp_regf.examples.simple.glbl.bus.WriteType
documentation:
Write Type.

Example Read/Write Operation Differentation.

Another Enumeration.

classes.name: ucdp_regf.examples.simple.glbl.bus.RespType
documentation:
Response Type.

Another enumeration for bus response.

classes.name: ucdp_regf.examples.simple.glbl.bus.BusType
documentation:
The Bus Type.

A struct which can handle forward and backward signals.

methods.name: ucdp_regf.ucdp_regf.UcdpRegfMod.add_soft_rst
documentation:
Add Soft Reset.

Calling w/o argument results in adding input 'soft_rst_i.
calling with a string '<word>.<field>' will use this field as soft reset.
Calling with any other string will be an error.
